# Ongoing 405 method not allowed incident (2026-01-26 17:16Z)

_**User**_

getting a 405 method not allowed from uptime monitor on the backend

---

_**Agent (model claude-4.5-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is reporting a 405 Method Not Allowed error from their uptime monitor. Looking at the screenshot, I can see:

1. It's an HTTP/S monitor for https://pense-backend.artpark.ai
2. The root cause is "405 Method Not Allowed"
3. The request shown is "HEAD http..." (partially visible)

The issue is that the uptime monitor is likely sending a HEAD request to the backend, but the backend doesn't have a route that accepts HEAD requests. Most uptime monitors use HEAD requests by default because they're lighter than GET requests.

Let me explore the codebase to understand the routing structure and find where I can add HEAD method support. I need to look at the main app entry point and routes.</details></think>

---

Looking at the screenshot, the uptime monitor is sending a HEAD request to your backend, which is returning 405 Method Not Allowed. This typically happens because the server doesn't have a route that explicitly handles HEAD requests.

Let me explore the codebase to understand the routing structure and find the best place to add HEAD method support.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="task_v2">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **task_v2**</summary>

```json
  "description": "Explore backend routes structure",
  "prompt": "Explore the pense-backend codebase to understand:\n1. What framework is being used (Express, Fastify, etc.)\n2. Where the main app entry point is\n3.   "subagentType": "explore",
  "model": "composer-1",
  "name": "explore"
}
```

format=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n@asynccontextmanager\\\\\\\\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Lifespan context manager for startup and shutdown events.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    # Startup: Initialize database and recover in_progress jobs\\\\\\\\n    init_db()\\\\\\\\n    logger.info(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Checking for in_progress jobs to recover...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n    recover_pending_jobs()\\\\\\\\n    yield\\\\\\\\n    # Shutdown: Nothing to do for now\\\\\\\\n    logger.info(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Application shutting down\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\napp = content_type: str  # e.g., \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"audio/wav\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"text/csv\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    extension: str  # e.g., \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wav\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", extension cannot be empty\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Validate task type\\\\\\\\n    if request.task_type not in [\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"stt\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", get_s3_output_config()\\\\\\\\n    except ValueError as e:\\\\\\\\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Generate UUID for unique file name\\\\\\\\n    file_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Construct S3 key: task_type/media/UUID.extension (no prefix)\\\\\\\\n    s3_key = f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{request.task_type}/media/{file_uuid}.{file_extension}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Generate presigned URL (expires in 1 hour)\\\\\\\\n    expiration = Summary\\\\n\\\\n**Framework:** FastAPI (Python)\\\\n\\\\n**Main Entry Point:** &#96;/Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-backend/src/main.py&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Health Check Route:**\\\\n- **Path:** &#96;GET /&#96;\\\\n- **Location:** Lines 114-116 in &#96;main.py&#96;\\\\n- **Implementation:**\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\n@app.get(\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\")\\\\ndef read_root():\\\\n    return {\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Health check successful!\\\\\\\"}\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Route Configuration:**\\\\n- Uses FastAPI's &#96;APIRouter&#96; for modular routing\\\\n- Routers are defined in &#96;src/routers/&#96; with prefixes (e.g., &#96;/auth&#96;, &#96;/users&#96;, &#96;/agents&#96;)\\\\n- Routers are included in &#96;main.py&#96; using &#96;app.include_router()&#96;\\\\n- Example router structure:\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\n  router = APIRouter(prefix=\\\\\\\"/auth\\\\\\\", tags=[\\\\\\\"auth\\\\\\\"])\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Additional Details:**\\\\n- Uses &#96;uvicorn&#96; as the ASGI server (from &#96;pyproject.toml&#96;)\\\\n- Includes CORS middleware configured via environment variables\\\\n- Has a lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown (database initialization and job recovery)\\\\n- Includes 13 routers: auth, users, agents, tools, agent_tools, stt, tts, tests, agent_tests, personas, scenarios, metrics, simulations, and jobs\\\\n\\\\nThe application follows standard FastAPI patterns with modular routing and a centralized main application Now I understand the issue. The backend uses FastAPI and the health check route only supports GET requests. The uptime monitor is sending HEAD requests, which FastAPI doesn't automatically handle for routes defined with `@app.get()`.

Let me read the main.py to see the current implementation.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see the issue. The health check route at `/` is defined with `@app.get("/")`, which only handles GET requests. The uptime monitor is sending HEAD requests, which returns 405.

The fix is simple - I need to add a HEAD method handler for the root route. In FastAPI, I can use `@app.head("/")` or use `@app.api_route("/", methods=["GET", "HEAD"])` to handle both methods.

The cleanest solution is to add a separate `@app.head("/")` decorator that returns a `Response` with no body (which is what HEAD requests expect - they should return the same headers as GET but with no body).

Let me add the HEAD handler for the health check route.</details></think>

---

I see the issue. The health check route at `/` only supports GET requests (`@app.get("/")`), but the uptime monitor is sending HEAD requests. I need to add HEAD method support for the root route.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-backend/src/main.py</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The change has been made. Now the health check route will respond to both GET and HEAD requests. For HEAD requests, FastAPI will automatically return just the headers without the body, which is the correct behavior per HTTP specification.

Let me verify the change was applied correctly.</details></think>

---

The fix has been applied. I've added `@app.head("/")` decorator to the health check route, so it now responds to both GET and HEAD requests.

For HEAD requests, FastAPI automatically returns the same headers as GET but without the response body, which is the correct HTTP behavior that uptime monitors expect.

Once you deploy this change, the uptime monitor should receive a 200 OK response instead of 405 Method Not Allowed.
